title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Hester and Philip engage in a conversation where Hester expresses her feelings of inadequacy, her history of suicidal ideation since age fourteen, and her resentment toward her mother. Hester describes her failed attempt to live independently and her failed romantic pursuit of an older, married man who lied to her. She reveals that she discovered she was adopted at age sixteen, which contributed to her sense of insecurity. Hester also expresses frustration that her mother was always right, which made Hester feel useless.

The conversation turns to the death of Hester's mother. Hester reveals that a man named Donald Craig believes she killed her mother and has offered to marry her if she confesses. Hester vehemently denies the killing, though she notes that others, including Mary and Coulston, also suspect her. Philip suggests that Hester should not marry Donald Craig, whom he dismisses as a pompous fool. As Philip kisses Hester to cheer her up, his wife, Mary Darlton, enters the room. Mary ignores Philip's attempts to explain the kiss or reconcile, eventually leaving the room after delivering a tray of soup.

In a separate scene, a butler informs Calgary that a pretty young lady wishes to see him. Calgary is surprised but instructs the butler to bring her up. Upon her arrival, Calgary recognizes the visitor and welcomes her into his room.
